description: Create the complete Python backtester (backtest.py) and data/README.md that simulates full NeuroX v9 trading logic against M1 OHLC CSV data, supporting both v9.1 (momentum only) and v9.2 (full filters) modes with comparison report output.
steps: Create data/README.md explaining the required CSV format (Date, Time, Open, High, Low, Close, Tick Volume, Volume, Spread) and how to export from MT5 (File > Open Offline > XAUUSD M1 > Export as CSV). Also mention auto-detection of common MT5 export formats.
Create backtest.py in repo root as a standalone script. Structure it with the following components:

(a) CSV parser: Read XAUUSD M1 CSV data. Auto-detect format - support both 'Date,Time,Open,High,Low,Close,TickVolume,Volume,Spread' and tab/comma variants. If Spread column is missing, default to 16 points (0.16). Handle date parsing for common MT5 date formats (YYYY.MM.DD, YYYY-MM-DD, etc).

(b) Backtester engine class with the following parameters from config.py: LOT_SIZE=0.01, COOLDOWN_SECONDS=5, MAX_HOLD_SECONDS=120, HYSTERESIS_THRESHOLD=0.50, MOMENTUM_LOOKBACK=8, MOMENTUM_THRESHOLD=0.60, HIGH_ATR_LOOKBACK=3, LOW_ATR_LOOKBACK=7, ATR_THRESHOLD_MULT=1.5, MAX_POSITIONS=5, STRONG_MOMENTUM_MULT=1.5, MIN_ATR_THRESHOLD=0.50, TICK_CONSISTENCY_LOOKBACK=40, TICK_CONSISTENCY_MIN_PCT=0.60, SIGNAL_PERSISTENCE_COUNT=2, REGIME_ATR_RATIO_THRESHOLD=0.8, REGIME_VARIANCE_RATIO_LOOKBACK=20, REGIME_VARIANCE_RATIO_THRESHOLD=0.5, MEAN_REVERSION_LOOKBACK=10, MEAN_REVERSION_ENTRY_PCT=0.20, MEAN_REVERSION_MIN_RANGE=1.00

(c) Momentum computation: Reuse or replicate logic from momentum.py - compute_momentum() with adaptive ATR-based lookback, compute_atr(), detect_regime() (ATR ratio + variance ratio), compute_mean_reversion_signal(). Import directly from momentum.py.

(d) Trailing stop simulation (4-tier system from EA): BE tier: profit >= $0.30, SL = entry + spread + $0.05 buffer (for BUY), entry - spread - buffer (for SELL). T2: profit >= $0.60, trail distance = $0.40 from current price. T3: profit >= $1.20, trail distance = $0.25. T4: profit >= $2.00, trail distance = $0.15. On each bar, update trailing SL (only tighten, never widen). Check if bar Low (for BUY) or bar High (for SELL) hits the SL.
(e) Fixed SL = $2.00 from entry. Check on each bar if price hits fixed SL.
(f) Hysteresis: Require $0.50 price move from last signal price to flip direction. Same-direction signals pass immediately.
(g) Position capacity: max 5 positions, additional positions only when momentum strength >= TICK_MOMENTUM_THRESHOLD * STRONG_MOMENTUM_MULT (0.30 * 1.5 = 0.45).

(h) Cooldown: 5 seconds (= 5 bars since we use M1 data where each bar = 60s, so cooldown is effectively ~0 bars or 1 bar depending on interpretation. Since M1 bars = 60s each, 5-second cooldown means same bar can open multiple trades. For backtesting, treat each bar as 60 seconds and allow 1 trade per bar as conservative approximation).
(i) Max hold time: 120 seconds = 2 bars on M1 data. Force close after 2 bars if position is still open.

(j) v9.2-only filters: ATR filter (skip if ATR < $0.50), tick consistency simulation (simulate from bar data - use directional consistency of recent closes over TICK_CONSISTENCY_LOOKBACK bars, since we don't have tick data), signal persistence (require 2 consecutive same-direction signals), regime detection (ATR ratio + variance ratio), mean reversion in ranging markets.

(k) v9.1 mode: Only momentum computation + hysteresis + position capacity + cooldown + max hold + trailing stops + fixed SL. No ATR filter, no tick consistency, no signal persistence, no regime detection, no mean reversion.

(l) Position tracking: Track each open position with entry price, entry bar index, direction, current trailing SL. On each bar, update all open positions (check SL hits, update trailing stops, check max hold).
(m) Trade execution simulation: Entry at bar Close (signal generated at bar close). Apply half-spread as slippage on entry and exit.

(n) Output comparison report showing for each mode (v9.1 and v9.2): Total trades, wins, losses, win rate, total profit, gross profit, gross loss, profit factor, max drawdown, average trade profit, best/worst trade, regime breakdown (trades in trending vs ranging - for v9.1 just show all as 'momentum').

(o) Main entry point: Accept CSV file path as command line argument. If no arg or file not found, print usage message and exit gracefully (exit code 0). Auto-run both v9.1 and v9.2 modes and print side-by-side comparison.
(p) Commission = $0 for all calculations. Spread from CSV if available, else 16 points (0.16 for XAUUSD). Lot size = 0.01 for all calcs.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'python backtest.py' with no arguments prints a usage message and exits with code 0
Running 'python backtest.py nonexistent.csv' prints an error message about file not found and exits gracefully
The script imports momentum.py functions (compute_momentum, compute_atr, detect_regime, compute_mean_reversion_signal) correctly
data/README.md exists and explains CSV format and MT5 export procedure
The backtester correctly simulates all 4 trailing stop tiers with the exact parameters specified
v9.1 mode skips ATR filter, tick consistency, signal persistence, regime detection, and mean reversion
v9.2 mode includes all filters and dual-mode regime detection

Output report includes all required metrics: total trades, wins, losses, win rate, total profit, gross profit, gross loss, profit factor, max drawdown, avg trade profit, best/worst trade, regime breakdown
verification: Run 'python backtest.py' and confirm it prints usage message and exits with code 0
Run 'python backtest.py nonexistent_file.csv' and confirm it shows a graceful error
Run 'python -c "import backtest"' to confirm the module imports without errors
Run 'python -m pytest tests/ -x --tb=short' to confirm existing tests are unaffected
findings: 1. pandas 3.0.3 read_csv with engine='python' works fine for auto-detection. 2. The Volume column from MT5 exports (Tick Volume) triggers volume-weighted momentum mode in compute_momentum() which is designed for tick-by-tick data; for bar backtesting, the Volume column is dropped before passing to momentum functions. 3. Column name deduplication was needed in the CSV parser - MT5 exports have both 'Tick Volume' and 'Volume' columns which could both map to 'Volume' causing pandas duplicate column issues. Solved by building output DataFrame directly instead of renaming in-place. 4. All 91 non-tick-collector tests pass unaffected. The tick_collector test failure is pre-existing. 5. Review fix pass: changed sys.exit(0) to sys.exit(1) for error paths (file not found, CSV parse failure); added comment documenting trailing-stop tier divergence from Config.TRAIL_TIERS; added comment explaining why Volume is dropped; added tick consistency approximation note in report footer.
